---
title: "Lexing the full Verilog-A keyword dictionary"
subtitle: "T1 · Phase T1.1 · `va-frontend::lexer`"
---

::: {.callout-note}
**Status: code complete, real coverage.** 20 tests, all green. Every reserved word LRM Annex D
lists is recognized (lowercase-only, per the LRM) — not just the tokens the bring-up model zoo
happens to use.
:::

## Goal

Turn Verilog-A source text into a token stream: recognize every reserved word and structural
symbol the language defines, without deciding anything about grammar yet.

## Where it fits

The first box in `CLAUDE.md` §2's pipeline — everything else in `va-frontend` (and, downstream,
`va-codegen`) starts from this token stream:

```
 Verilog-A source
        │
   [lexer] ──tokens──► [parser] ──AST──► [elaborate] ──IR (Interface α)──► va-codegen
```

## The idea

Built on [`logos`](https://docs.rs/logos), a derive-macro lexer generator: each `Token` variant
carries its own `#[token(...)]`/`#[regex(...)]` pattern, and `logos` compiles the whole set into
one DFA. Three design choices matter more than the mechanics:

**Full reserved-word coverage, not a hand-picked subset.** Every dedicated structural keyword
(`module`, `analog`, `if`, `parameter`, …) gets its own `Token` variant, so the parser can match
on shape instead of re-comparing strings; every *other* LRM-reserved word — including every
math/analog built-in (`exp`, `ddt`, `idt`, `transition`, …) — is carried as one generic
`Token::Keyword(Keyword)`, classified by name later. `V`/`I` (the access functions) are
deliberately **not** reserved: they lex as ordinary identifiers and are only recognized as access
functions contextually, since real corpus files sometimes declare a plain variable or parameter
that happens to share a keyword-like name (§ the `vt`/`temperature` un-reservation,
`docs/roadmap.md`'s language-coverage backlog).

**Numeric literals fold their own SI scale suffix at lex time**, not later: `3.3Meg`/`10n`/`1k`
all resolve straight to a scaled `f64` (`T G M K k m u n p f a`, matching SPICE/Verilog-A scale
letters — note `M`/`m` are case-sensitive, mega vs. milli).

**Completeness is checked by construction, not by manual enumeration.** `crate::keywords`
maintains one canonical `RESERVED_WORDS` list (LRM Annex D); a single test loops over it and
asserts every entry lexes as *something other than* a plain identifier — so a keyword nobody
remembered to wire a `#[token(...)]` for fails a real test, rather than silently lexing wrong
the first time a corpus file happens to use it.

## The code

```rust
#[derive(Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Logos)]
#[logos(skip r"[ \t\r\n\f]+")]                    // whitespace
#[logos(skip r"//[^\n]*")]                        // line comment
#[logos(skip r"/\*[^*]*\*+([^/*][^*]*\*+)*/")]     // block comment
#[logos(skip r"\(\*[^*]*\*+([^)*][^*]*\*+)*\)")]   // (* attribute *) — discarded
pub enum Token {
    #[regex(r"[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*", |lex| lex.slice().to_string())]
    #[regex(r"\\[!-~]+", |lex| lex.slice()[1..].to_string())]   // \escaped identifier
    Ident(String),

    #[regex(r"[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?([eE][+-]?[0-9]+)?[TGMKkmunpfa]?", parse_number)]
    Number(f64),

    #[regex(r#""([^"\\]|\\.)*""#, parse_string)]
    Str(String),

    #[token("module")] Module,
    #[token("analog")] Analog,
    // … every other dedicated structural keyword …

    #[token("exp", kw)] /* … ~200 more reserved words, all routed through `kw` … */
    Keyword(Keyword),
}
```

`kw` is the same one-line callback for every generic reserved word:

```rust
fn kw(lex: &logos::Lexer<Token>) -> Keyword {
    Keyword::from_ident(lex.slice()).expect("matched lexeme is a reserved word")
}
```

An escaped identifier (`` \cpu3 ``, LRM §2.8.1 — backslash through the next whitespace) lexes
into the **same** `Token::Ident` variant as a plain one, just via a second regex on the same
token — genuinely interchangeable from every later stage's point of view, since both produce
identical output.

## It works

```console
$ cargo test -p va-frontend lexer
running 20 tests
test lexer::tests::comments_are_skipped ... ok
test lexer::tests::every_reserved_word_is_reserved ... ok
test lexer::tests::numbers_scientific_and_scaled ... ok
test lexer::tests::string_escapes_are_resolved ... ok
test lexer::tests::escaped_identifier_swallows_punctuation_up_to_whitespace ... ok
test lexer::tests::whole_models_lex_without_error ... ok
...
test result: ok. 20 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; 0 measured; 196 filtered out; finished in 0.00s
```

`whole_models_lex_without_error` runs the lexer end-to-end over the project's own zoo models
(`resistor.va`/`capacitor.va`/`diode.va`), the same files `va-core` solves; `numbers_scientific_and_scaled`
and `string_escapes_are_resolved` were both added chasing a real corpus failure, not written from
a spec in isolation — the string-escape fix in particular came from `bsimsoi.va`'s error-message
string embedding an escaped quote, which broke the *original* naive `"[^"]*"` regex and took
every subsequent token in the file down with it.

## Limitations

Stated in the lexer module's own doc comment: `` `include ``/`` `define `` lex as opaque
`Token::Directive` tokens (expanded by a separate preprocessing pass, `preprocess.rs`, not the
lexer itself); numeric literals need a leading digit (`0.5`, not `.5`); sized/based integer
literals (`` 4'b0101 ``) are out of scope — none of these are digital-Verilog constructs Annex C
excludes, they're just not needed by any Verilog-A analog model in the corpus surveyed so far.

## What's next

`t1-frontend/02-parsing.qmd` — turning this token stream into an AST.
